user.title: SmartHomeShop.io
user.description:
Add a SmartHomeShop product to Home Assistant.

Select the product that matches your physical device. The device must already be online in Home Assistant through the ESPHome integration.

data_description.product_type: Choose the SmartHomeShop product you want to configure.

device.title: Select {product_name}
device.description:
{product_description}

Select the ESPHome device that belongs to this product.

water_options.title: Leak detection
water_options.description:
These sensors were found on your device:
- Water usage: `{water_sensor}`
- Flow rate: `{flow_sensor}`

Leak detection watches these sensors and adds alert entities to Home Assistant. Pick settings that match your household. You can change everything later via **Configure**.

data_description.continuous_flow_minutes: Alert when water keeps flowing non-stop for this long. This catches a burst pipe, a forgotten garden hose or a running toilet. 30 minutes is a good default; choose longer if you often fill a bath or pool.
data_description.night_start: From this time on your home normally uses little to no water. Unexpected usage during the night window is treated as a possible leak.
data_description.night_end: End of the night window. After this time, normal daytime usage applies again.
data_description.vacation_mode_entity: Optional: pick a toggle helper (input_boolean) that you switch on when you are away. While it is on, any water usage immediately raises a leak alert.

summary.title: Ready to add
summary.description:
**{product_name}** on **{device_name}** is ready.

The following entities will be added to Home Assistant:

{entities}

All entity names end in *(CC)* so you can tell them apart from the built-in ESPHome entities.

error.no_water_sensor: No water sensor found on this device

abort.no_devices_found:
No devices of this type found.

Make sure your SmartHomeShop device is added to Home Assistant via the ESPHome integration and is online.

For setup instructions, visit: docs.smarthomeshop.io
abort.invalid_link_request: This link request is not valid
abort.no_water_sensor: No water sensor was found on this device. Make sure the device is online and its water sensors are enabled in Home Assistant, then try again.

data_description.continuous_flow_minutes: Alert when water keeps flowing non-stop for this long. This catches a burst pipe, a forgotten garden hose or a running toilet. 30 minutes is a good default; choose longer if you often fill a bath or pool.
data_description.night_start: From this time on your home normally uses little to no water. Unexpected usage during the night window is treated as a possible leak.
data_description.night_end: End of the night window. After this time, normal daytime usage applies again.
data_description.vacation_mode_entity: Optional: pick a toggle helper (input_boolean) that you switch on when you are away. While it is on, any water usage immediately raises a leak alert.
data_description.price_electricity_t1: What you pay per kWh at the normal/day tariff. Used for the cost sensors.
data_description.price_electricity_t2: What you pay per kWh at the low/night tariff.
data_description.price_feed_in: What you receive per kWh that your solar panels feed back into the grid.
data_description.price_gas: What you pay per m³ of gas.
data_description.price_water: What you pay per m³ (1000 litres) of water. Used for the water cost sensor.
data_description.main_fuse_amps: The size of your main fuse per phase (see your meter cupboard, e.g. 25A or 35A). Used for the phase load sensor.

leak_detected.title: Possible water leak on {device}
leak_detected.description:
The leak detection of **{device}** reports a possible leak (type: {leak_type}).

Check your taps, toilet and appliances. This issue disappears automatically once water usage is back to normal.
